title: Mima II

{{Quote|Bilars are found on Mima II, a small, tropical world orbiting the orange sun Lar.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
'''Mima II''', also referred to as '''Mima''', was a tropical [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]]. The [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] influence of [[Lar]], the planet's [[Star/Legends|star]], caused the tectonic plates on Mima II to be constantly moving across its surface in various directions, leading to the active creation of new landforms, such as [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] and [[Ocean/Legends|seas]]. The accompanying release of chemical elements vital to the development of life resulted in the planet being abundant with [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal [[Species/Legends|species]], including the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Bilar]]s.

Living in Mima II's lush [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] as well as in [[City/Legends|cities]], the Bilars were [[Diet/Legends|herbivorous]] [[Primate/Legends|primates]] that had developed a unique ability to form [[Hive mind/Legends|group minds]] known as [[claqa]]s. That ability, coupled with the abundance of basic necessities on Mima II, such as [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], allowed the Bilars to rapidly establish a highly technological society on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. However, due to various other reasons, interstellar [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] avoided doing business with the planet.

==Description==
===Geologic activity===
{{Quote|Due to Mima II's proximity to Lar, Lar's gravitational influence draws the planet's tectonic plates over its molten core in much the same way a standard planet's moon draws tides across its oceans. As Mima II orbits its star, the plates are pulled in different directions, constantly raising new mountain ranges, forming new land masses, opening new seas, creating new volcanoes, and so forth.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:DAelgoth-EAOC.jpg|thumb|left|240px|Mima II was located in the D'Aelgoth sector of the Mid Rim.]]
Mima II,<ref name="CSWE" /> also known simply as Mima,<ref name="Zorba" /> was a small terrestrial<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Lar system]],<ref name="CSWE" /> a part of the [[D'Aelgoth sector/Legends|D'Aelgoth sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of<ref name="TEA Lar" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Mima II occupied the second position in its [[Star system/Legends|system]]<ref name="CSWE" /> and [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] the [[Color/Legends|orange]] [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Lar]]<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> at a relatively short distance.<ref name="GG42" /> In turn, the planet was orbited by at least two [[Moon/Legends|moons]].<ref name="Zorba" /> Mima II had a fast rotation of fifteen [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]] and a short [[Standard year/Legends|year]] that only lasted 103 [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]].<ref name="GG42" />

Due to the proximity of Mima II to Lar and the [[Gravity/Legends|gravitational]] influence of the latter on the former,<ref name="GG42" /> Mima II's tectonic plates were highly unstable and were drawn across the planet's surface<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> and over its molten core, exhibiting a similarity to moons creating tidal activity on other planets. Depending on Mima II's position relative to its star, the tectonic plates were pulled in various directions, and such movement and collisions between the plates constantly created new landmasses, [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]] ranges, [[Volcano/Legends|volcanoes]], and [[Ocean/Legends|seas]] on the planet's surface.<ref name="GG42" />

===A world rich in life===
{{Quote|Great jungles of yellow, blue, and even green plants tower hundreds of meters above the ground, creating a biosphere with dozens of distinct levels. Hanging vines and clinging lianas cascade down the steep slopes of Mima II's youthful mountains. Where the oceans are too deep for plants to root, buoyant carpets of yellow moss blanket the surface.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:Bilar2_GG4.jpg|thumb|right|220px|The sentient Bilars were among the species that lived in the jungles on Mima II.]]
The high [[Geology|geological]] activity of Mima II released abundant amounts of [[Carbon/Legends|carbon]], [[Hydrogen/Legends|hydrogen]], [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]], and other substances vital for the development of living organisms.<ref name="GG42" /> The intense [[electromagnetic radiation]] emitted by Lar transformed these chemical elements into a variety of organic molecules, which, combined with the extremely [[Temperature/Legends|warm]] climate of the tropical world, led to an abundance of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal life on the planet.<ref name="GG41" />

In regions of Mima II's oceans that were too deep for plants to root, carpets of buoyant yellow [[Moss/Legends|moss]] blanketed the [[Water/Legends|water's]] surface. On land, clinging [[Liana (vine)|lianas]] and hanging vines grew on the steep slopes of the young mountains of the planet. In lower elevations, raging rivers alternated with great, verdant [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] of blue, green, and yellow vegetation and [[Tree/Legends|trees]] that reached hundreds of [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height. The jungles were thick and murky, had dense brush, and constituted a biosphere that had dozens of distinct levels. Mima II's fauna included the [[riam]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] flying [[Snake/Legends|snake]].<ref name="GG42" />

==History==
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
At some point around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="Date TOR">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "Liquidation Slae" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the game, the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> a [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquake]] on Mima II resulted in one of the planet's business districts being sent on a slow slide into a [[Magma/Legends|magma]] pool. In response, an individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Galactic War|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] sent a companion to the planet in order to salvage anything valuable from the district before it plunged into magma. When the companion arrived on site, the district's slide had temporarily slowed down, and the individual carried out a thorough search of the businesses located therein.<ref name="SWTOR" />
{{Endgame}}

[[File:Zorba_in_Holiday_Towers.jpg|thumb|left|250px|In 5 ABY, individuals from the moons of Mima II were present at the Holiday Towers Hotel and Casino in Cloud City.]]
The [[sentientologist]] [[Obo Rin]] included Mima II and its [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] inhabitants, the [[Bilar]]s, in the ''[[Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy]]''. In that work, Rin described the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] species that he considered to be the most important in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and of the most interest to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="GG42" />

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]],<ref name="5 ABY">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Zorba the Hutt's Revenge]]'' to [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]].</ref> affluent businesspeople from the moons of Mima II were among the various [[Gambling/Legends|gamblers]] present at the [[Holiday Towers Hotel and Casino]] in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], a floating [[City/Legends|metropolis]] located in the [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] of the<ref name="Zorba" /> [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]].<ref name="Zorba" />

==Inhabitants==
{{Main|Bilar}}
{{Quote|Amazingly, the kings of Mima II's jungles are the Bilars—pink-skinned primates equally at home scurrying through the dense brush or swinging by their gangly arms through the jungle canopy.|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
[[File:Bilargg4.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Bilars established a technological society on Mima II.]]
Mima II was the homeworld of the Bilars. A sentient species of herbivorous [[Primate/Legends|primates]], Bilars were well-adapted for life in Mima II's jungles. The Imperial sentientologist Obo Rin described the Bilars as the "[[King/Legends|kings]]" of their homeworld's jungles due to their unique ability to form [[claqa]]s, a type of [[Hive mind/Legends|hive mind]] shared by groups of Bilars that amplified their intelligence and senses.<ref name="GG42" />

Pacifistic and carefree, Bilars had little experience with deprivation due to basic necessities, such as [[Foodstuff/Legends|food]], being abundantly present on their homeworld. The latter factor also led to the species developing in a short [[Time/Legends|time]] a society of a sophisticated technological level with an emphasis on mass production.<ref name="GG42" />

However, for various other reasons, such as Mima II having little to offer in the way of goods not available elsewhere in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], most interstellar [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] avoided doing business with Mima II. As a result, most Bilars were confined to their homeworld, as they could not afford [[Transport/Legends|transportation]] off-world. In addition, despite some of the larger claqas, such as [[Unni Yerudi]], being able to find a way to leave Mima II, most of the Bilars that left the planet eventually returned due to the ease of life there as opposed to the rest of the galaxy.<ref name="GG42" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|Most claqas of five units or more prefer to live in cities (located in the central portions of Mima II's tectonic plates in order to minimize damage from plate movement and collision).|Obo Rin, ''Catalog of Intelligent Life in the Galaxy'', Revised Edition|<ref name="GG42" />}}
By 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]],<ref name="Date TOR" /> Mima II was home to several business districts. In the aftermath of a local groundquake around that time, one of the districts of a [[Unidentified city (Mima II)|city]] on Mima II was sent sliding toward a magma pool.<ref name="SWTOR" /> A portion of Mima II's native Bilars lived in cities built in the central areas of the planet's tectonic plates, which were relatively safe from the world's violent geological activity. Large cities on Mima II featured parks with trees planted in them.<ref name="GG42" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ImperialCore-Voyages13.png|thumb|right|200px|Map from ''Voyages SF'' 13 depicting Mima II as part of the "Imperial Core."]]
Mima II was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races]]'', a [[1989]] sourcebook authored by [[Troy Denning]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="GG41" /> "[[Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are]]," a [[1990]] [[roleplaying game]] source article published in the [[Voyages SF 13|thirteenth issue]] of the ''[[Voyages SF]]'' magazine, placed the planet in the "Imperial Core" [[Sector/Legends|sector]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Twinkle">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Lar system, and therefore Mima II, in grid square L-17, relatively far from the galaxy's [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]],<ref name="TEA Lar" /> and the [[StarWars.com]] [[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion|Online Companion]] to the book further overrode the ''Voyages SF'' 13 placement by establishing that the system was situated in the D'Aelgoth sector.<ref name="EAOC" />

''[[Zorba the Hutt's Revenge]]'', a [[1992]] young-reader novel written by [[Paul Davids|Paul]] and [[Hollace Davids]], introduced the shortened form of the planet's name, "Mima."<ref name="Zorba" /> The alternate spelling of the planet's name, "Mima Two," was later introduced in a Scavenging Crew Skill mission of the [[2011]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]''. The information regarding the mission's outcome was eventually removed from the game, however.<ref name="SWTOR" />

The [[1998]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' claims that the tectonic activity on Mima II was caused by a nearby planet named Lar,<ref name="Encyclopedia" /> which contradicts the first edition<ref name="GG41" /> and the [[1994]] [[Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races (Second Edition)|second edition]] of ''Galaxy Guide 4: Alien Races'', both of which state that it was the star Lar that exerted its gravitational influence on Mima II.<ref name="GG42" /> This article assumes that the ''Star Wars Encyclopedia'' is in error.

==Appearances==
